chat.author: Steffen Fries
chat.text: Hi Toerless, regarding the status, BRSKI-PRM is in MISSREF, Mahesh pushed es forward.

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: If I remember it correctly, the brski-prm draft was dependent on rfc8366bis. Since that document is now in IESG Evaluation state, it was a good time to let the brski-prm draft go into the RFC Editor queue.

chat.author: Steffen Fries
chat.text: Yes true, thanks for pushing it forward.

chat.author: Toerless Eckert
chat.text: Not sure if anyone else beside Steffen would need a say, sounds like a go ahead for Mahesh.

chat.author: Esko Dijk
chat.text: GRASP through routers: we would go from zero-config to "minimal-config - but not zero" , it seems?

chat.author: Titouan Soulard
chat.text: @Esko: totally right. One-per-subnet configuration is very minimal: relays know each other, but can be discovered by nodes. Omniscient configuration has more configuration: each node knows at least one relay.
